qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CSRF protection disabled on GraphQL API enables cross-origin mutation execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes disabled CSRF verification on Decidim GraphQL mutations, not the private data export file relation or UUID collision root cause from the advisory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe constantize on user-controlled GraphQL type argument enables arbitrary class resolution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes GraphQL commentable type constantization and arbitrary model lookup, not Decidim private exports, ActiveStorage attachment record_id coercion, or UUID prefix collisions.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory concerns Decidim private data exports where Decidim::PrivateExport uses an ActiveStorage file attachment whose active_storage_attachments.record_id bigint storage coerces UUID IDs to integers, causing collisions and possible cross-user export file exposure. The qualifying findings instead cover GraphQL CSRF and GraphQL unsafe constantization.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-3cx6-j9j4-54mp describes private data export leaks caused by UUID-to-integer collisions in ActiveStorage attachment record_id handling for Decidim::PrivateExport.file.
graphql_api_csrf/vulnerability_report.json describes skip_before_action :verify_authenticity_token and cross-origin GraphQL mutation execution.
graphql_unsafe_constantize/vulnerability_report.json describes user-controlled GraphQL type.constantize.find(id) in Decidim comments query and mutation extensions.
A session search of final artifacts found no qualifying report evidence for Decidim::PrivateExport, private_exports, download_your_data, ActiveStorage record_id conversion, or UUID collision.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
